summary: An abstract of a prospective cohort study co-authored by Frank B. Hu examining the association between dietary amino acids and stroke risk. The study concludes that higher intakes of glutamine (found in plant protein and whole grains) and proline (found in dairy) are associated with lower risks of ischemic and total stroke. The source is a standard scientific abstract and does not contain extractable frameworks, mental models, or signature quotes regarding the author's broader thought process.
